function_status_label: Which labeled relation is a function?
Which panel shows exactly one y-value for every x-value in its domain?
Which labeled graph represents y as a function of x?
Which labeled relation assigns a single output y to each input x?
Which relation never pairs the same x-value with two different y-values?
one_to_one_status_label: Which labeled relation represents a one-to-one function?
Which panel shows a function with no repeated y-values?
Which panel shows a function where each y-value occurs at most once?
Which labeled relation maps different x-values to different y-values?
domain_match_label: Which panel shows a relation whose domain is {target_domain}?
Find the labeled graph with x-values spanning {target_domain}.
Which relation uses exactly the domain {target_domain}?
range_match_label: Which panel shows a relation whose range is {target_range}?
Find the labeled graph with y-values spanning {target_range}.
Which relation uses exactly the range {target_range}?
y_axis_symmetry_label: Which labeled graph is symmetric about the y-axis?
Which panel shows a relation with mirror symmetry across the y-axis?
Find the relation that stays unchanged when reflected across the y-axis.
Which labeled relation has y-axis symmetry?
Which panel contains a graph symmetric across the vertical axis?
x_axis_symmetry_label: Which labeled graph is symmetric about the x-axis?
Which panel shows a relation with mirror symmetry across the x-axis?
Find the relation that stays unchanged when reflected across the x-axis.
Which labeled relation has x-axis symmetry?
Which panel contains a graph symmetric across the horizontal axis?
origin_symmetry_label: Which labeled graph is symmetric about the origin?
Which panel shows a relation unchanged by a 180-degree rotation about the origin?
Find the relation with origin symmetry.
Which labeled relation has rotational symmetry about the origin?
Which panel contains a graph symmetric through the origin?
monotonic_interval_increasing_label: Which labeled graph is increasing on {target_interval}?
Which panel shows a graph that increases across {target_interval}?
Find the labeled graph that rises from left to right on {target_interval}.
Which relation is increasing throughout the interval {target_interval}?
Which panel contains a graph with y-values increasing as x moves through {target_interval}?
monotonic_interval_decreasing_label: Which labeled graph is decreasing on {target_interval}?
Which panel shows a graph that decreases across {target_interval}?
Find the labeled graph that falls from left to right on {target_interval}.
Which relation is decreasing throughout the interval {target_interval}?
Which panel contains a graph with y-values decreasing as x moves through {target_interval}?
sign_interval_positive_label: Which labeled graph stays above the x-axis on {target_interval}?
Which panel shows a graph with positive y-values throughout {target_interval}?
Find the graph that is positive on the interval {target_interval}.
Which relation remains above y = 0 for all x in {target_interval}?
Which panel contains a graph whose y-values are positive across {target_interval}?
sign_interval_negative_label: Which labeled graph stays below the x-axis on {target_interval}?
Which panel shows a graph with negative y-values throughout {target_interval}?
Find the graph that is negative on the interval {target_interval}.
Which relation remains below y = 0 for all x in {target_interval}?
Which panel contains a graph whose y-values are negative across {target_interval}?

code_prompt_defaults.object_description: labeled coordinate-plane panels, each showing one relation
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_selected_panel_bbox: set "annotation" to a JSON array containing the selected panel bounding box as [x0,y0,x1,y1] in pixels
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_option_letter: set "answer" to the single selected panel label as a capital letter shown in the image
